title: NZ Easter School Holidays 2027 — Family Ideas, Dates, Events & Costs
meta_desc: Plan the NZ Easter school holidays 2027 with kids — exact dates, top autumn destinations, Easter events and egg hunts, what's open, and what it costs.
intro: Easter 2027 lands in early autumn — the sweet spot of the NZ travel year, when the weather is settled, the crowds have thinned, and a four-day long weekend rolls straight into the autumn school holidays. With Good Friday on 2 April and Easter Monday on 5 April, plus the school break running roughly 9–25 April, families get a generous window for a road trip or a relaxed regional escape. This guide covers exact dates, the best Easter destinations, events and egg hunts, what's open over the restricted-trading days, and what it all costs.
key_takeaway: Easter 2027: Good Friday 2 April, Easter Monday 5 April. The autumn school holidays run approx 9–25 April 2027. Easter is cheaper and quieter than summer but still busy — book 6–8 weeks ahead. Good Friday and Easter Sunday are restricted trading days, so stock up beforehand.

sections.heading: Easter 2027 Dates and School Holidays
sections.body: Easter 2027 falls slightly earlier than 2026. Good Friday is 2 April and Easter Monday is 5 April 2027, giving a four-day long weekend. The autumn school holidays then run approximately 9–25 April 2027 (regions vary by a few days, and Term 1 ends around 8 April). For many families the long weekend and the school break almost join up, making this one of the best stretches of the year for a getaway.

sections.heading: Why Easter Is a Great Time to Travel with Kids
sections.body: Easter sits in early autumn, when NZ weather is often at its most settled — warm, calm days and cool nights, with far fewer crowds than summer. The four-day long weekend means you don't have to burn annual leave for a proper break, and prices haven't hit summer peaks. It's also the last realistic window for warm-enough beach days in the upper North Island before the cooler months set in.
list: Four-day long weekend (Good Friday to Easter Monday) with no leave required
Settled autumn weather — golden light, mild 15–20°C days
Smaller crowds and lower prices than summer
Autumn colour in Central Otago, harvest season in the wine regions
Plenty of family events — egg hunts, A&P shows, autumn fairs

sections.heading: Top NZ Easter Destinations for Families
sections.body: These regions are at their best over Easter — mild weather, family-friendly activities, and good rainy-day backups if autumn delivers a wet weekend.

rows: Last of the warm beach weather; calm bays
Hot Water Beach, Cathedral Cove, quieter than summer
Harvest season, family wineries with playgrounds
Autumn colour, luge, biking, lake activities

sections.heading: Easter Events and Egg Hunts
sections.body: Easter weekend is packed with family events across the country. Most are free or low-cost, and many holiday parks run their own hunts for guests.
list: Botanic garden and farm-park egg hunts in all main centres
Zoo and wildlife-park Easter trails (Auckland Zoo, Wellington Zoo, Orana Wildlife Park)
Easter A&P shows and autumn fairs in rural towns
Chocolate festivals and Easter markets (check local listings closer to the date)
Holiday-park organised egg hunts and kids' activities for guests

sections.heading: Family-Friendly Wineries in Marlborough
sections.body: Easter is harvest season in Marlborough, and several cellar doors are genuinely set up for families — with playgrounds, lawns, and platters the kids will actually eat. It's a relaxed way to enjoy the region while the grown-ups taste a few wines.
list: Look for cellar doors with dedicated lawns, playgrounds, and kids' menus
Pair a tasting with a picnic and a run-around for the kids on the lawn
Combine with the Marlborough Sounds — water taxis, easy walks, and calm beaches
Blenheim's Pollard Park and the Omaka Aviation Heritage Centre are great non-winery options

sections.heading: What's Open Over Easter (Trading Restrictions)
sections.body: Good Friday and Easter Sunday are restricted trading days in NZ — many shops must close by law, though cafes, restaurants, petrol stations, pharmacies, and most tourist attractions can stay open. Supermarkets are closed Good Friday and Easter Sunday morning, so stock up beforehand. Easter Saturday and Easter Monday both trade normally.
list: Closed Good Friday & Easter Sunday: most retail shops and supermarkets
Open: cafes, restaurants, petrol stations, pharmacies, most attractions and tours
Trade normally: Easter Saturday and Easter Monday
Tip: do your grocery shop on Thursday or Easter Saturday to cover the closed days

sections.heading: Easter vs Summer — Cost Comparison
sections.body: Easter is noticeably cheaper than the summer school holidays, but it's far from a quiet weekend — the long weekend is one of the busiest travel periods of the year. Expect prices between off-peak and summer peak.

sections.heading: Booking Tips for the Long Weekend
sections.body: The Easter long weekend books up fast despite being cheaper than summer. A little planning goes a long way.
list: Book accommodation 6–8 weeks ahead — popular holiday parks and baches fill quickly
If you want a campervan, reserve early; autumn fleets are smaller than summer
Do your supermarket shop before Good Friday to cover the closed trading days
Travel out on Thursday evening or early Friday to beat the long-weekend traffic
Pack layers — autumn days are warm but evenings get cold, especially inland

sections.heading: Plan the Rest of 2027
sections.body: Easter is just one of four NZ school holidays. See the full NZ school holiday dates 2025–2027, the winter school holidays activities guide, and the summer 2026/27 family guide. For a calendar view of every break, visit the NZ school holidays hub.

faq.question: When is Easter 2027 in New Zealand?
faq.answer: Good Friday is 2 April 2027 and Easter Monday is 5 April 2027, giving a four-day long weekend. The autumn school holidays then run approximately 9–25 April 2027, with regions varying by a few days.

faq.question: Are the Easter school holidays a good time to travel with kids?
faq.answer: Yes — the four-day long weekend, settled autumn weather, and lower prices than summer make Easter one of the best-value times for a family trip. The long weekend itself is busy, so book accommodation 6–8 weeks ahead, especially for popular holiday parks and baches.

faq.question: What's open over Easter weekend in NZ?
faq.answer: Good Friday and Easter Sunday are restricted trading days — most shops and supermarkets close, but cafes, petrol stations, pharmacies, and most tourist attractions stay open. Supermarkets close Good Friday and Easter Sunday morning, so shop beforehand. Easter Saturday and Easter Monday trade normally.

faq.question: Where should we go for Easter in NZ with kids?
faq.answer: Great autumn picks include Northland and the Bay of Islands (last warm beach weather), the Coromandel, Marlborough (harvest season with family wineries), Queenstown and Central Otago (autumn colour), and Rotorua or Taupo for rain-safe geothermal fun.

faq.question: How much does an Easter family holiday in NZ cost?
faq.answer: Expect roughly $150–300 per day for a family of four — more than off-peak but cheaper than the summer holidays. Camping and self-catering sit at the lower end; cabins, motels, and paid attractions push it higher.

faq.question: How far ahead should I book for Easter?
faq.answer: Book accommodation 6–8 weeks ahead. The Easter long weekend is one of the busiest travel periods of the year, and the best holiday parks, baches, and campervans fill quickly even though prices are lower than summer.

email_capture.heading: Get NZ Family Trip Ideas Emailed Monthly
email_capture.body: New itineraries, activities, and family travel guides sent to your inbox on the first of each month. Plus exclusive tips for traveling with kids in NZ.
